findings.description: User-controlled display_name and action are f-stringed into HTML with zero escaping, so a user named with a <script> tag yields stored XSS executing in the admin's privileged dashboard session.

PAGE_TEMPLATE.format() runs over the user-derived 'rows' string, so any display_name/action containing a literal { or } triggers KeyError/IndexError (DoS) or unintended re-interpolation, and 'title' is likewise injected unescaped.
The docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the SQL uses created_at < end (half-open), silently dropping the entire end boundary day from the report.
Building the rows HTML via repeated string concatenation (rows += ...) in a loop is O(n^2)-ish for large event sets; a list-accumulate-then-join would be the non-lazy choice.
